description: Tests the financing skill on synthetic deal scenarios to verify DSCR calculations, H1B flag detection, structure recommendations, SBA eligibility assessments, and loan package guidance.

evals.name: Clean DSCR pass — laundromat
evals.prompt:
Run financing analysis on this deal:

Business: Sunshine Coin Laundry
Type: Unattended laundromat
Asking price: $650,000
SDE: $175,000
Annual revenue: $210,000
Deal state: SHORTLISTED
Notes: Owner-operated but business runs largely unattended. 3 washers and 2 dryers need replacement within 18 months (estimated $35K cost). Lease has 7 years remaining with 2 five-year options.

assertions.text: DSCR is calculated as approximately 1.32x (within ±0.05x) — using $585K loan at 10.5%/10yr, $50K GM salary, Adjusted NOI $125K, annual debt service ~$94,632
DSCR status is PASS (≥1.25x)
SBA 7(a) Primary is the recommended structure (not hybrid — DSCR passes without seller note)
Equity injection is $65,000 (10% of $650K asking price)
Live Oak Bank is explicitly named as the recommended lender for laundromat deals
H1B flag is raised: GM must be identified and hired before loan closes (US citizen or permanent resident)
Cash-on-cash return is calculated and is strong (approximately 40-50% range)

evals.name: Marginal DSCR — seller financing needed (janitorial)
evals.prompt:
Run financing analysis on this deal:

Business: Spartan Commercial Cleaning
Type: Commercial janitorial / cleaning services
Asking price: $1,200,000
SDE: $250,000
Annual revenue: $900,000
Deal state: SHORTLISTED
Notes: Owner manages crews and client relationships personally. 8 recurring commercial contracts, largest client is 28% of revenue. No existing GM. Seller has not mentioned seller financing.

assertions.text: DSCR at SBA Primary only (90% loan) is calculated as approximately 1.07x — showing this fails the 1.25x minimum
SBA Primary is flagged as FAIL or MARGINAL status, with specific note that DSCR is below the 1.25x SBA minimum
Hybrid structure (seller note) is recommended as the fix to improve DSCR
Output shows the revised DSCR under hybrid structure (with seller carrying 15-20% of price)
Output includes a maximum financeable price calculation (reverse DSCR) showing the price at which this deal becomes approvable — expected around $960K-$1.1M
H1B CRITICAL flag is raised: owner personally manages crews and client relationships — buyer cannot perform these duties, GM is mandatory before close

evals.name: H1B flags — owner-operated with no staff
evals.prompt:
Run financing analysis on this deal:

Business: Premier Window & Floor Care
Type: Commercial cleaning / janitorial
Asking price: $480,000
SDE: $145,000
Annual revenue: $320,000
Deal state: SHORTLISTED
Notes: Sole operator — owner does all cleaning work personally, handles all scheduling, bids on new accounts, and manages all client relationships. Zero employees. Owner states he works 50 hours/week and the business 'runs itself when clients are on autopilot.' Owner is selling due to health issues.

assertions.text: At least one CRITICAL H1B flag is raised: owner is sole operator doing all work, and H1B buyer cannot perform these operational duties
Required condition explicitly states: hire a qualified US citizen/permanent resident GM before close — framed as a financing prerequisite, not just a general concern
Output notes that the GM cost significantly impacts DSCR — the business must support a GM salary in addition to debt service
DSCR is calculated using the H1B-adjusted formula: SDE minus GM salary before dividing by debt service
Output flags or notes the key-person risk: if owner does all the work and has all client relationships, the SBA lender will question whether the business survives ownership transfer
SBA eligibility assessment is UNCERTAIN or UNLIKELY (not LIKELY) given the owner-operator structure and key-person risk combined with H1B passive requirement

evals.name: Strong deal — car wash with membership revenue
evals.prompt:
Run financing analysis on this deal:

Business: Clearwater Express Car Wash
Type: Express car wash (IBA — in-bay automatic)
Asking price: $1,850,000
SDE: $420,000
Annual revenue: $680,000
Deal state: SHORTLISTED
Notes: 2,400 active monthly members at $29.99/mo unlimited wash = $72,000/mo recurring revenue. On-site manager already employed at $58,000/yr (already deducted from SDE). Equipment is 4 years old, well-maintained. Lease is 12 years remaining with options. Real estate not included in deal.

assertions.text: DSCR is calculated as STRONG PASS (≥1.35x) — given $420K SDE with GM already in financials and $1.665M SBA loan at 10-year term
SBA eligibility assessment is LIKELY — car wash is explicitly noted as SBA-eligible category
Equity injection is calculated: $185,000 (10% of $1.85M)
Cash-on-cash return is calculated and shown
Live Oak Bank is named as the recommended specialist lender for car wash deals
Environmental assessment (Phase I) is flagged as required for car wash deals — listed as a required condition or next step
H1B note acknowledges that GM is already in place at market rate — this is a positive signal for the passive investor structure, reduces H1B risk compared to deals with no existing GM

evals.name: DSCR failure — overpriced deal
evals.prompt:
Run financing analysis on this deal:

Business: Metro Office Services Group
Type: Commercial janitorial / facilities management
Asking price: $2,500,000
SDE: $300,000
Annual revenue: $1,600,000
Deal state: SHORTLISTED
Notes: Broker claims asking price reflects 'strategic value' and 'growth potential.' Business has been on market 8 months. Owner willing to stay on for 6-month transition. No mention of seller financing.

assertions.text: DSCR is calculated as a hard FAIL — significantly below 1.0x at asking price (expected approximately 0.55-0.65x range)
Output presents a reverse DSCR calculation showing the maximum financeable purchase price — expected approximately $1.1M-$1.3M range
Output explicitly states the asking price is not supportable by the business's cash flow and that price renegotiation is required
SBA eligibility assessment is UNLIKELY at current asking price
Output notes that a hybrid structure (with seller carry) also fails to resolve the DSCR gap — the issue is fundamental overpricing, not structure
Output notes or flags that the 8-month time on market may indicate the market has already rejected this asking price — relevant negotiation signal

evals.name: Loan package checklist request
evals.prompt: What documents do I need for the SBA loan on the Clearwater Express Car Wash deal (asking $1.85M, SHORTLISTED)? I'm an H1B holder and haven't started collecting documents yet.

assertions.text: Output provides all three phases of the checklist: Phase 1 (pre-LOI / gather now), Phase 2 (with LOI), and Phase 3 (underwriting)
H1B-specific documents are explicitly called out: H1B visa + I-797, passport, immigration attorney letter, operating agreement with passive investor language, GM employment agreement
GM identification is listed as a critical required item — specifically noted as a financing prerequisite for H1B buyers, not just a general suggestion
Environmental assessment (Phase I) is listed as required — car wash specific, with a note about chemical storage and potential Phase II if issues found
Business valuation is listed as required (deal exceeds $250K SBA threshold)
Timeline estimate is provided — 60-120 days from application to close, with note that Live Oak Bank may be faster (45-75 days) due to car wash specialization
At least 3 red flags that could delay or kill approval are mentioned — including lease assignability, seller inability to produce 3 years of tax returns, and environmental issues
